desc:
Built on the western shores of Aman, facing only the Encircling Sea and the Wall
of the Night stand the vast Halls of Mandos. In this small and remote corner of
the Halls of Awaiting you tarry in a time of recollection, perhaps listening to
echoes of times long gone by, or perhaps dreaming dreams of your own and
wondering when the time will come that you are able to return once more to roam
freely upon Middle-earth.
name: Halls of Awaiting

desc:
Vast and strong are the Halls of Awaiting, tall are the Pelori, called also the
Mountains of Defence, and long and mysterious is the Straight Way to the True
West, and yet still sometimes rumour reaches this place of the deeds and words
of those who still dwell upon Middle-earth.
name: Halls of Awaiting

desc:
Half-substance, half-shadow, high walls rise to the distant roof of the Halls.
Far above, just below the roof, many narrow windows have been cut in the shadowy
fabric. Through these windows golden and silvery light enters the Halls and
strange scents and whispers drift in. Some of these whispers speak of strange
lands and things; others are in no language of Middle-earth that you can
recognise.
name: Halls of Awaiting

desc:
In this corner of the Halls of Awaiting you might hear echoes and rumours of
things long gone by, or catch a glimpse of more recent events on Middle-earth.
For though you are far removed here from the events in the mortal realm, still
like reflections and dreams their tales sometimes reach you here.
name: Halls of Awaiting

desc:
The floor in this part of the wide halls slopes down until it turns into a wide
pool of silvery water. The pool's surface is still and shining like a polished
mirror, yet deep down it is filled with a faint light. The light seems clearer
and cleaner than anything you have ever seen. Sometimes it seems the light would
come from within of the pool's water itself; these silver reflections are only
faint, but still bright and blinding as the highest sun.
name: The Silver Pool

desc:
Where once a haggard team of grizzled old builders toiled under the watchful eye
of Arion, their slavemaster, to create the old Dwarven Halls of Khazad-dum, now
an energetic group of bright-eyed young builders and supervisors (and a few old
farts) strive to recreate Lothlorien, that elven land of fabled beauty and only
rumoured peril.
name: Lothlorien

desc:
You stand on a bald hill surrounded in all directions by thick, dark forest.
From beneath the distant branches, dim, heavy rustlings, groans, and chitterings
can be heard. Far to the west, beyond the Anduin, a pale golden haze hangs above
a much brighter-looking forest. The hilltop is covered with great cyclopean
stones, each marked with its own identifying number. Orcs, organized in
work-gangs, bustle about with loads of wood, cloth, stone, precious metals, and
household goods, snarling and cursing as they bump into each other. Some very
large trolls, seemingly immune to the rays of the dim sun, sullenly trudge past
carrying huge shovels. A wooden lean-to contains a drafting table, on which,
weighted with metal chunks to keep them from unrolling, are some parchment rolls
on which architectural drawings can be seen.
name: Southern Mirkwood Construction Co.

desc:
An ominous claustrophobic sense envelops you as you try and navigate the
twisted, dense undergrowth that makes the best of the gloomy conditions below
the canopy of the endless forest.
name: Mirkwood

desc:
Welcome to the forest called Fangorn, bordered on the east by the Wold, and on
the west by the high peaks of the southern Misty Mountains. Elvish history tells
how, when Varda, Queen of the Heavens, rekindled the Stars and awoke the Elves
the Ents also awoke in the Great Forests of Arda. They came from the thoughts of
Yavanna, Queen of the Earth, and were her shepherds of trees. Shepherds and
guardians they proved to be, for if roused to anger Entish wrath was terrible
and they could crush stone and steel with their hands alone. Justly they were
feared, but they were also gentle and wise. "Do not be too hasty, that is my
motto." - Treebeard A small sign is hanging on a nearby branch.
name: Fangorn Forest [No Smoking Please]

desc:
Mysterious stains spot the lime, corduroy upholstry of this spacious couch.
Small crumbs and misplaced cigarette ash offer a granular texture to the
otherwise smooth surface of the fabric. Between the ill-fitting green cushions
are gaping black chasms leading into the perilous depths of the seat. A freshly
starched blanket is neatly folded on the right arm of the couch.
name: A Giant Green Sofa

desc:
You are emersed in a big bowl of comfortably warm oatmeal. Squished around the
soggy oats are large blocks of sugar cubes, stacked into comfortable resting
chairs and sofas. A thin trickle of warm milk pours into the bowl from a nearby
granite spigot, which constantly refreshes the temperature and causes thick,
slow-rising bubbles to gently surface and pop, releasing warm brown-sugar
scented vapours into the steamy air.
name: Staer's Big Bowl of Porridge

desc:
Several Finns are in this hot dimly lit room and everyone is holding a cold
bottle of beer sipping from it every now and then. Everyone is uttering in some
strange tongue. The air in this room is hot and humid. There is a stove full of
steaming black stones in the middle of the room while the walls are made of huge
logs. There is a pile of used bath whisks made of birch branches. The cute arse
of Muumi is hanging on the wall. A small window is in the east wall with a view
to a lake covered with ice and a hole in it. A finn's head is peeking above the
dark waters in the hole. There is a lovely oil painting hanging above the stove.
A huge thermometer is hanging on the wall. Some czech is here suffering from the
heat.
name: Sauna

desc:
Here, hidden from the ever-watchful eyes of people, Staer conducts his secret
dealings and powers with the unknown. Mysterious creatures live and die within
the confines of these wet, rock hardened walls. The air lights up with zips of
blue and yellow - as mudlle magic inundates the air.
name: Staer's Private Lab

desc:
Darkness surrounds this fire-lit forest glen, where four logs have been placed
in a square around a blazing bonfire. Beyond the fire's perimeter, recflections
of eyes of all kinds of shapes and sizes weave in and out of the inky darkness.
Hissing, scraping sounds can be heard from somewhere close by, but just as the
ear begins to discern one sound, it stops, just to be replaced by another.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
A cozy-looking, red summer cottage stands in the warm, bright sunlight upon a
smooth rock of the lakeside. The lake is as deep blue as a sapphire, and its
water look refreshing and tempting. Smoke is curling up from the chimney of the
cottage - it looks like Antti is heating up sauna for his Finn, Gnome and Troll
friends. Behind the cottage, a peaceful birch forest sings a soft, relaxing
melody, a praise for the sun and the tender summer wind!
name: Antti's Summer Cottage near Warrens

desc:
The Dark Lord's servants have reached Barad-dur with the Ring. The holocaust
begins. Suddenly the earth groans and quakes! Rising swiftly up, high above the
mountains, vast soaring darkness springs into the sky, flickering with fire. His
voice, dim but growing, filled with ancient evil, harsh as stone, spoke the
words of betrayal: Ash nazg durbatuluk, ash nazg gimbatul, Ash nazg thrakatuluk
agh burzum-ishi krimpatul! From the south in the land of Mordor, there rose a
huge shape of shadow, black against the pall of cloud, impenetrable,
lightning-crowned, filling all the sky. Enormous it reared above the world, on
its hand a band of utter darkness, dooming the land to night eternal.
name: The Epilogue

desc:
Cool wisps of fresh air sweep calmly over this picturesque landscape. The
overwhelmingly peaceful and serene atmosphere undoubtedly reflect the character
of the Ainur that resides here. Close to the waters edge, there is an inviting
canopy providing just enough shelter from the warm sun. White sand soft as
powder covers the majority of this coastline dotted only by the occasional palm
tree.
name: Anne's Private Beach

desc:
Several Finns are in this hot dimly lit room and everyone is holding a cold
bottle of beer sipping from it every now and then. Everyone is uttering in some
strange tongue. The air in this room is hot and humid. There is a stove full of
steaming black stones in the middle of the room. The walls are made of huge
logs. There is a pile of used bath whisks made of birch branches. The cute arse
of Muumi is hanging on the wall. A small window is in the east wall with a view
to a lake covered with ice and a hole in it. A finn's head is peeking above the
dark waters in the hole. There is a lovely oil painting hanging above the stove.
name: Sauna

desc: ororor
name: Humala's place of Pleasure

desc:
You find yourself surrounded by ancient walls that have crumbled in spots. The
ground has been dug up by what appear to have been numerous battles raging here
for many years. Magic must have been released here since no plantation is
capable of growing in this area. Only one being was victorious throughout all
the wars and it occasionally visits here to gloat. In the corner sits a
magnificent Parlour Palm. Humala, wielding an engraved lag hammer, is sitting
here on your face (wizinvis M and idle). Humala, wielding an engraved lag
hammer, is sitting here on your face (wizinvis M and idle). Humala, wielding an
engraved lag hammer, is sitting here on your face (wizinvis M and idle). Humala,
wielding an engraved lag hammer, is sitting here on your face (wizinvis M and
idle). Humala, wielding an engraved lag hammer, is sitting here on your face
(wizinvis M and idle). Humala, wielding an engraved lag hammer, is sitting here
on your face (wizinvis M and idle).
name: Presidential Palace of Peru

desc:
This is a luxurious room, obviously used for the relaxation of the Ainur. Soft
sofas line the walls, their plush fabric inviting you to lie down. A bar is in
the centre of the room. Many crystal containers of wines and liquors are set
neatly across the top. The contents look very inviting. A big bottle of Dain's
Laphroaig stands atop the bar. A cask of Maedhros's current favourite, Longrow,
stands ready for tasting.
name: The Gods' Antechamber

desc:
This is where builders come and fix their typos, if they want to not be accused
of being lazy dogs. A special warning scrawled in blood from Maedhros says:
Please, please, please remove the post you correct! (And if possible mail the
poster of the report.)
name: Tpyo board

desc:
The walls in this chamber are made of white marble, being circular in shape,
alike to those of a tower. Several, wide pillars of stone travel high above,
where they greet and support the ceiling. A spiral, silver staircase traces
close to the pillars, until the stairs disappear beyond sight above, where a
blazing light enters the room. The staircase is held aloft by several, thin rods
that are also silver, and each one is reflecting a great amount of the light
that shines in from above. The lower portion of this chamber lies in shadow
though, where there is only a single beam of light that reaches down and upon a
sculpture of Nimloth the White Tree of Numenor. The sculpture of the tree stands
in the center of the chamber, near the base of the stairs. Between the pillars
are several paintings, each one was painted directly onto the walls. Though this
chamber is only a small space within the Halls of Doom, this is where Almarian,
loyal servant of Namo, can be found after her tasks are complete for the day,
painting all of Numenorean's sorrow upon the walls.
name: Vestige of Numenor

desc:
This golden abode is the home of Namo, one of the Feanturi. Large pillars rise
towards the bejewelled ceiling, covered with ancient paintings depicting Arda
during its early days. Eru and Manwe can easily be recognized, as well as
Yavanna and the golden trees, Telperion and Laurelin. The cold floor has been
engraved with strange patterns, giving this room an eerie feeling. Fabulous
wooden benches has been put along the walls, each bench in front of a pillar,
making it easy for you to sit down and watch the beautiful paintings. But then,
who would want to spend time here? The Halls of the Doomed, the Halls of the
Dead. A place every mortal will have to pass through, strong or weak, beautiful
or ugly. The mere thought makes you dizzy.
name: The Halls of Doom

desc:
There is a large, oaken table on this green meadow. On the table are numerous
detailed models of cities, environment, and landscapes. There are trees, rivers,
even entire cities but most of the models are covered with a thick layer of
dust. Yet in some places, the dust has been removed and it seems that someone
made a few changes here and there, adding new, shiny spots.
name: Coordination Meadow for New Quests in Old Zones

desc:
A warm summer night embraces Antti's summer cottage, blanketing it into soft
shades of dark, wrapping everything within its protective arms, providing
peaceful and safe sleep. The beautiful lake around the cottage is totally still,
only a vague sound of some night bird can be heard in the distance. A group of
Sauna gnomes are dancing on the smooth rock before the cottage.
name: Antti's Summer Cottage near Warrens
